meaning: n.电报机,电报v.打电报,发电报,打电报说
examples: Now I must telegraph your father to come.
A new telegraph line has been set up between the two cities.
The telegraph, the telephone, and radio were the obvious predecessors of the Internet, iPods, and mobile phones.
The pie-throwing attacker is a comedian who calls himself Jonnie Marbles, according to The Telegraph.
For newspapers such as the Sunday Telegraph the test seems to be much simpler.
